item.title: Should My Husband Tell His Best Friend That We Can’t Stand His Wife?
item.description: His friend has accused him of being distant, even hinting that he’s a social climber for not wanting to hang out with them.

item.title: Is There a Founding Story That Can Unify Left and Right?
item.description: There has never been agreement over the meaning of America’s creation 250 years ago. Maybe there shouldn’t be.

item.title: Should You Let Your Dog Fertilize Your Lawn?
item.description: A ruling on a dispute over proper mowing technique.

item.title: Robby Hoffman Will Always Feel Poor, No Matter How Rich She Gets
item.description: The comedian and actor says class and the way she grew up inform everything about the way she lives now.

item.title: My Awful Mother Is Getting Alzheimer’s. What Do I Owe Her?
item.description: The thought of having her live with me makes me physically sick.

item.title: Did American-Style ‘Gentle Parenting’ Spoil French Children?
item.description: As positive parenting takes over France, one psychologist’s call for a return to discipline has set off a furious debate.

item.title: Why Is Florida Executing So Many Prisoners?
item.description: In most of the country, executions are a thing of the past. But one state has been carrying them out at a record pace.

item.title: How A.I. Might Change the Way Doctors Think
item.description: For generations, writing up a summary of a patient exam was a vital step for physicians trying to make an accurate diagnosis. What happens when A.I. does it for them?

item.title: Is a ‘Frictionless’ Society a Trap?
item.description: Eliminating whatever stands between you and a task sounds nice, but it probably has more to offer companies than users.

item.title: My Sister Insists That She Can’t Work. Should I Pay Her Bills?
item.description: I want to help, but I worry financial support may enable her rather than help her.

item.title: Our Bacteria Are Talking. We’ve Just Begun to Understand What They’re Saying.
item.description: The human microbiome is essential to our health, but scientists still know very little about it. Two researchers set out to map this largely uncharted terrain.

item.title: I Think the Workmen at My Condo Are Being Exploited. What Should I Do?
item.description: Many of us suspect they are undocumented, and being underpaid.

item.title: World Cup Tourists Are Giving Americans Exactly What We Crave
item.description: TikTok de Tocqueville have descended on our Walmarts, Buc-ees and Bass Pro Shops. Our delight at their content says a lot about us.

item.title: Has There Ever Been a Better Time to Be a ‘Degenerate’?
item.description: It used to be a nasty expression of contempt. Now all sorts of people laughingly self-identify as degenerate.
